COVID-19 update for July 15-17: Vax registration opens for B.C. kids six months up to 5 years | Cases rise but people in intensive care and dying drops   Here's your daily update with everything you need to know on the coronavirus situation in B.C. and around the world.

B.C. urges parents of babies and toddlers to get a COVID vaccine after Health Canada approval   Pediatric expert says the benefits of the vaccine outweighs its risks. The province expects to start immunizing children six months old to four years old on Aug. 2 and says parents can register their children now
Vancouver weather: Sun and clouds leading into the weekend   Clouds start to move in today, with a 40 per cent chance of showers tonight.

Scammers using social media to get information to defraud Vancouver seniors, police say   A Kerrisdale woman in her 90s gave a fake courier $12,000 cash thinking it was being used to bail her grandson out of jail

Ivana Trump, first wife of Donald Trump, dies at 73   The couple married in 1977 and divorced in 1992. They had three children together: Donald Jr., Ivanka and Eric.

Court documents detail RCMP investigation into Edmonton MLA's hacking of Alberta vaccine system   In a statement, UCP government whip Brad Rutherford called the number of hacking attempts “shocking” and suggested the NDP has been misleading Albertans in terms of what they knew

Canada bringing back mandatory random testing of travellers arriving at main airports   Testing will resume as of July 19 for fully vaccinated travellers arriving at the Vancouver, Calgary, Montreal and Toronto airports.
Winnipeg man accused of making, trafficking 3D gun   Winnipeg Police are warning that more and more 3D guns and the parts to create those guns are making their way onto the city’s streets

Texas sues to block federal guidance on abortions to save mother's life   Abortion services ceased in Texas after the state’s highest court on July 2 cleared the way for a nearly century-old abortion ban to take effect.
Canadian dollar hits 20-month low on global risk aversion   Equities globally and the price of oil, one of Canada’s major exports, fell

Stocks dodge an inflation wipeout: Explaining the tepid reaction   Data that landed like a gut punch on anyone hoping inflation had receded was received with notable poise by investors. Why that happened was the subject of frantic speculation Wednesday on Wall Street.

Loonie's disconnect from oil prices makes higher interest rates necessary: Tiff Macklem   Canadian dollar hasn't gone up with oil prices like it usually does, complicating the Bank of Canada's inflation fight
Bank of Canada's jumbo rate hike set to slow lenders' earnings growth   Mortgages accounted for about 50% of Canadian banks’ loans book, analysts estimate.

How a teen 'vampire's' blog blew up a murder trial — and prevented a wrongful conviction   The blog contained a description of her 'likes,' which included: 'Blood, pain ... rain, darkness, thunder ... fire, my bedroom, sex ... cemeteries, knives'

True Crime Byline: How one childhood incident pushed Dawn Crey into a downward spiral from which she would never escape   The new podcast True Crime Byline looks back at The Vancouver Sun investigation into Vancouver's missing women, a case that eventually led to the arrest and conviction of Robert Pickton. This profile of missing woman Dawn Crey was part of a series of investigative stories published in 2001.

True Crime Byline: A Politician Murdered his Ex Wife. We Should Have Seen it Coming   Looking back on the murder of JoAnn Wilson today, what's most surprising is that we were surprised it happened at all.

Canucks' superhero-themed game opening video is finalist for industry award   The Minnesota Vikings took the title for their video, but the quality of the Canucks' clip, directed by the team's in-game entertainment boss Mike Pohorly, had them in serious contention

Vancouver Canadians slugger Damiano Palmegiani happily hammering homers in his hometown   The Surrey third baseman was drafted originally by the Toronto Blue Jays in 2018 out of high school. He opted to go to college instead and was drafted again by the Blue Jays out of junior college in 2021

Kevin Spacey pulls out of Genghis Khan film after sex assault charges   He has denied the allegations after travelling from the U.S. to Britain to contest the allegations.

Zimmers Hole among those gearing up to shred at Armstrong Metal Festival   Nearly 30 acts will shred eardrums at the 12th edition of the live extreme music festival on July 15 and 16.

Restaurant review: Riley's Fish & Steak serves up the classic chop house experience — with a twist   Restaurateur Emad Yacoub is looking to fill what he feels is a void in the Vancouver food scene with his latest restaurant: Riley's Fish & Steak.

Pulling Together: An eight-day Shuswap canoe journey drawing in Indigenous, law enforcement and public service communities   "For me, it's heartwarming to see like-minded individuals come together for the betterment of Indigenous communities and their relationships with public services." — Kyle Crump, a youth recreation manager with the Splatsin in Enderby

Some tomatoes fare better than the rest in hot weather   A fertile, humus-rich soil kept thoroughly and consistently moist is a basic first step toward stress-resistant tomato plants.

Community group asks City of Vancouver to oppose rezoning after B.C. Housing review and board firings   The Kitsilano Coalition said Vancouver should ask B.C. Housing and its new board to review and make changes to the Arbutus social housing project.

Hyundai unveils high-performance electric N concepts   They likely won't go into production, but they'll help engineers develop technologies that will

The 2023 Toyota Crown is a lifted Japanese luxury sedan   Call it the return of the prodigal car: Toyota’s flagship is coming back to Canada, exclusively as a premium hybrid

GM, travel operator Pilot to develop EV charging network   The companies will develop 2,000 EV charging stalls at travel centres
Healthy Eating: Happy National Cherry Day   Cherry pits are generally safe to eat, even though they contain amygdalin, a chemical that the body can convert into cyanide.
How do I know if have a sinus infection?   If you have blocked nose, or pressure around your face and eyes, bad breath or pain with chewing, you may have a sinus infection.

As Told To: 'Not everyone knows how to react to someone with a life-altering injury'   But you can start by not raising your voice or feeling the need to speak slower, says Russ Davies, who has been living with a brain injury since 2009: 'People think you aren't capable of having a conversation.'

What are the signs of cataracts?   While there is currently no known way to prevent cataracts, you can reduce your risk by wearing sunglasses, eating foods rich in antioxidants, and getting regular eye exams.

WATCH: #HowieMandel breaks TikTok with a photo of an anal prolapse   The America’s Got Talent judge took over TikTok last weekend by posting a photo of a severe anal prolapse — which aren't as rare as we'd like them to be.

Former Pfizer site in Puerto Rico leased to a cannabis company   “We require many of the same types of machinery and outfitting — strong security systems, clean rooms, testing facilities, laboratories."
Ontario driver found unconscious behind the wheel faces a number of drug charges   A 40-year-old man from Kenora was arrested following a tip from the public.

American Israeli woman formerly jailed in Russia over cannabis talks about Brittney Griner's case   Naama Issachar had about nine grams of cannabis in her possession and recalls feeling her heart sink when a Russian airport official discovered a small plastic bag tucked inside a fanny pack.
Man from PEI lands a four-year prison sentence for drug trafficking   Summerside man pleads guilty to possessing cocaine, methamphetamine and cannabis following a traffic stop bust.

Joe Rogan responds to border drug seizure: ‘You should take all these drugs and rethink your life’   Border agents seized small amounts of ecstasy, cannabis and psilocybin mushrooms from a vehicle entering New York.
